raw_response: I agree with the conclusion reached by Agents 1 and 2. The key point here is the anatomical distinction between different types of molars concerning their number of cusps. Specifically, the mandibular second molar consistently exhibits only four cusps instead of the standard five observed in many other molars including the mandibular first, third, and even some maxillary first molars. This unique characteristic makes it clear why Option A correctly identifies the tooth lacking five cusps. My agreement supports maintaining confidence in our selected answer choice A because it aligns precisely with established dental knowledge about molar morphology. Hence, my final answer remains consistent: A. Mandibular Second Molar.
